---
title: "3 · Physics — Part 2: Hydromechanics"
---

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**Scope.** The mechanical state and its link to water: near-surface stiffness (Vs30, NEHRP), and the
petrophysical effective-medium relation by which water changes velocity — poroelastic pore pressure
(saturated) and dynamic capillarity (vadose), measured by ambient-noise dv/v.
:::

*Content migrating from "Mechanical modeling grounds" and "Petrophysical linking models" (the
effective-medium derivation, acoustoelastic β, dynamic capillary stress).*

## Mechanical modelling grounds (Vs30, tomography, NEHRP)

## Petrophysical link: velocity, stiffness, and water

## Saturated zone — pore pressure (poroelastic + acoustoelastic)

## Vadose zone — partial saturation and dynamic capillarity
